title: Gianluca Atzori

infobox.value: (born 6 March 1971) is an Italian [football](/wiki/Football_in_Italy "Football in Italy") manager and former player, currently in charge of [Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D") club [Scafatese](/wiki/Scafatese_Calcio_1922 "Scafatese Calcio 1922").

["Gianluca Atzori è il nuovo allenatore del Catania"](https://web.archive.org/web/20100620210201/http://calciocatania.net/comunicati/comunicati.php?id=16586) (in Italian). Calcio Catania. 10 June 2009. Archived from [the original](http://www.calciocatania.net/comunicati/comunicati.php?id=16586) on 20 June 2010. Retrieved 10 June 2009.

["Sinisa Mihajlovic è il nuovo allenatore del Catania"](https://web.archive.org/web/20100620224300/http://calciocatania.net/comunicati/comunicati.php?id=18160) (in Italian). Calcio Catania. 8 December 2009. Archived from [the original](http://calciocatania.net/comunicati/comunicati.php?id=18160) on 20 June 2010. Retrieved 8 December 2009.

["Atzori alla Reggina. Lunedi' la presentazione"](https://web.archive.org/web/20161018230249/http://www.regginacalcio.com/index.php?option=com_content&task=view&id=2415&Itemid=15) (in Italian). Reggina Calcio. 16 June 2010. Archived from [the original](http://www.regginacalcio.com/index.php?option=com_content&task=view&id=2415&Itemid=15) on 18 October 2016. Retrieved 17 June 2010.
["Gianluca Atzori è il nuovo tecnico"](http://www.sampdoria.it/index.php?option=com_content&task=view&id=8269&Itemid=38) (in Italian). U.C. Sampdoria. 9 June 2011. Retrieved 9 June 2011.

["U.C. SAMPDORIA: COMUNICATO STAMPA 13 NOVEMBRE 2011"](https://web.archive.org/web/20111115234911/http://www.sampdoria.it/news/uc-sampdoria-comunicato-stampa-13-novembre-2011.html) (in Italian). [U.C. Sampdoria](/wiki/U.C._Sampdoria "U.C. Sampdoria"). 13 November 2011. Archived from [the original](http://www.sampdoria.it/news/uc-sampdoria-comunicato-stampa-13-novembre-2011.html) on 15 November 2011. Retrieved 13 November 2011.

["LA REGGINA RIPARTE DA GIANLUCA ATZORI"](https://web.archive.org/web/20130621033352/http://www.regginacalcio.com/news/archivio-stagione/7370-la-reggina-riparte-da-gianluca-atzori.html) [REGGINA STARTS AGAIN WITH GIANLUCA ATZORI] (in Italian). Reggina Calcio. 17 June 2013. Archived from [the original](http://www.regginacalcio.com/news/archivio-stagione/7370-la-reggina-riparte-da-gianluca-atzori.html) on 21 June 2013. Retrieved 6 July 2013.

["Reggina: in panchina torna Atzori"](https://web.archive.org/web/20131214132658/http://www.gazzetta.it/notizie-ultima-ora/Calcio/Reggina-panchina-torna-Atzori/03-12-2013/2-A_004417164.shtml) [Reggina: Atzori back on the bench] (in Italian). Gazzetta dello Spo

**Gianluca Atzori** (born 6 March 1971) is an Italian [football](/wiki/Football_in_Italy "Football in Italy") manager and former player, currently in charge of [Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D") club [Scafatese](/wiki/Scafatese_Calcio_1922 "Scafatese Calcio 1922").

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gianluca_Atzori&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

### Playing

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gianluca_Atzori&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Playing")]

Atzori started his professional playing career with Rome-based Serie C side [Lodigiani](/wiki/A.S._Lodigiani "A.S. Lodigiani") in 1988, then playing mostly at Serie B level with several teams, including [Perugia](/wiki/Perugia_Calcio "Perugia Calcio"), [Reggina](/wiki/Reggina_Calcio "Reggina Calcio") and most notably [Ravenna](/wiki/Ravenna_Calcio "Ravenna Calcio"), where he spent a total four season. In 2001, he joined [Empoli](/wiki/Empoli_F.C. "Empoli F.C."), being a protagonist of the Tuscans' successful [2001–02 Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B_2001%E2%80%9302 "Serie B 2001–02") campaign which saw the team being crowned league winners under head coach [Silvio Baldini](/wiki/Silvio_Baldini "Silvio Baldini"). He then played another season with Empoli in his personal first (and only) Serie A season. In 2003, he followed Silvio Baldini at [Palermo](/wiki/U.S._Citt%C3%A0_di_Palermo "U.S. Città di Palermo"), who later won the Serie B title in 2003–04; he played only 17 league games out of 46 with the *rosanero*, being relegated to the bench after Silvio Baldini's dismissal from the head coaching post.

### Coaching

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gianluca_Atzori&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Coaching")]

After being released by Palermo in June 2004, Atzori chose to retire from football and instead focus on a management career, becoming [Silvio Baldini](/wiki/Silvio_Baldini "Silvio Baldini")'s assistant at [Parma](/wiki/Parma_F.C. "Parma F.C."). He then followed again Baldini also in his unsuccessful stints at [Lecce](/wiki/U.S._Lecce "U.S. Lecce") (2005–06) and [Catania](/wiki/Calcio_Catania "Calcio Catania") (2007–08), both ended in his boss and mentor being sacked in mid-season. However, after Baldini was sacked from Catania in April 2008, Atzori decided instead to stay under new boss [Walter Zenga](/wiki/Walter_Zenga "Walter Zenga").[[1]](#cite_note-sportal-1)

In June 2008, Atzori left Catania to accept a head coaching offer from [Lega Pro Prima Divisione](/wiki/Lega_Pro_Prima_Divisione "Lega Pro Prima Divisione") side [Ravenna](/wiki/Ravenna_Calcio "Ravenna Calcio"), a team where he had already spent four seasons as a player. He guided Ravenna through a very impressive season and qualification into promotion playoffs, where Ravenna was defeated by [Padova](/wiki/Calcio_Padova "Calcio Padova") in the semi-finals.[[1]](#cite_note-sportal-1)

On 10 June 2009, Atzori accepted to return to Catania, this time as head coach, for the upcoming [2009–10 season](/wiki/Serie_A_2009%E2%80%9310 "Serie A 2009–10"), thus replacing his former head coach Walter Zenga at the helm of the *rossoazzurri*.[[1]](#cite_note-sportal-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) However, his time back at Catania lasted only fifteen games, as he was dismissed on 8 December due to poor results.[[3]](#cite_note-mihajlovic_catania-3)

On 16 June 2010 [Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B") club [Reggina](/wiki/Reggina_Calcio "Reggina Calcio") announced Gianluca Atzori as the club's new head coach, replacing [Roberto Breda](/wiki/Roberto_Breda "Roberto Breda").[[4]](#cite_note-reggina-4)

On 9 June 2011, Atzori was named as the new head coach of [Sampdoria](/wiki/U.C._Sampdoria "U.C. Sampdoria") have been relegated to [Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B"), signing a two-year contract.[[5]](#cite_note-sampdoria-5) After a disappointing start of season, with Sampdoria in seventh place despite being tipped as one of the leading direct promotion candidates during pre-season, Atzori was ultimately sacked on 13 November 2011.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

On 4 January 2013, he was named the new coach of [Spezia](/wiki/Spezia_Calcio "Spezia Calcio") in [Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B") in place of the sacked [Michele Serena](/wiki/Michele_Serena "Michele Serena"). His stint was, however, short-lived as he was sacked weeks later due to poor results and replaced by [Luigi Cagni](/wiki/Luigi_Cagni "Luigi Cagni").

In June 2013, he was named the new head coach of the Serie B club [Reggina](/wiki/Reggina_Calcio "Reggina Calcio"), thus marking a personal second stint at the Calabrian club.[[7]](#cite_note-7) He was sacked in October 2013 and replaced by [Fabrizio Castori](/wiki/Fabrizio_Castori "Fabrizio Castori"), but reinstated in December 2013.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

He successively served as head coach of [Serie B](/wiki/Serie_B "Serie B") club [Pro Vercelli](/wiki/F.C._Pro_Vercelli_1892 "F.C. Pro Vercelli 1892") from December 2017 to January 2018, in a short but unsuccessful stint in charge of the *Bianche Casacche*.

On 27 September 2019 he was hired by [Serie C](/wiki/Serie_C "Serie C") club [Imolese](/wiki/Imolese_Calcio_1919 "Imolese Calcio 1919").[[9]](#cite_note-9) He left Imolese at the end of the 2019–20 season.

On 17 June 2021 Atzori has been appointed as head coach of the [Maltese Premier League](/wiki/Maltese_Premier_League "Maltese Premier League") side [Floriana](/wiki/Floriana_F.C. "Floriana F.C.").[[10]](#cite_note-10) On 10 May 2023, after two years with the Maltese club during which he won the [2021–22 Maltese FA Trophy](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_Maltese_FA_Trophy "2021–22 Maltese FA Trophy"), Atzori parted ways with Floriana.[[11]](#cite_note-11) He successively declared to have left the club due to unpaid wages, not having received a salary since January 2023, despite still having another year of contract with Floriana.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

On 2 December 2024, Atzori returned to Italy as the new head coach of ambitious [Serie D](/wiki/Serie_D "Serie D") amateurs [Scafatese](/wiki/Scafatese_Calcio_1922 "Scafatese Calcio 1922").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gianluca_Atzori&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Honours")]

### Manager

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gianluca_Atzori&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Manager")]

[Floriana](/wiki/Floriana_F.C. "Floriana F.C.")

* [Maltese FA Trophy](/wiki/Maltese_FA_Trophy "Maltese FA Trophy"): [2021–22](/wiki/2021%E2%80%9322_Maltese_FA_Trophy "2021–22 Maltese FA Trophy")
